description: Route the two raw config.json readers through the migration-aware loader, preserving their degraded contracts, so no reader sees a different config shape than loadConfig once a shape-changing config migration ships. resolveOperationVocabulary (pi-agent-dispatch capability-composer, active substrate, fail-safe to TOOL_OPERATION_DEFAULTS) reads via loadConfigForDir inside its existing guards; loadConfigForDirBestEffort (pi-context context-sdk, foreign substrates feeding the porcelain id index, best-effort to null) delegates to loadConfigForDir in try/catch. Any load failure — absent, unparsable, unresolvable version, invalid — collapses to the same degraded value each contract already defines; a lagging-but-chained config now reads its migrated shape.
acceptance_criteria: resolveOperationVocabulary reads the active substrate's config through the migration-aware loader: a current valid config with tool_operations overrides yields those overrides; a config whose schema_version has no resolvable chain to the bundled schema yields TOOL_OPERATION_DEFAULTS (fail-safe), never the raw un-migrated shape.

loadConfigForDirBestEffort delegates to the migration-aware loader with the null contract preserved: an absent, unparsable, or unresolvable foreign config returns null and the foreign-index porcelain degrades without throwing; a valid foreign config returns its migrated shape.

Tests cover the unresolvable-version-to-degraded-value cell and the current-config-passthrough cell at both sites; build, check, and the full test suite pass at zero failures with the change in dist/.
notes: Resolves the gap's filed determination toward migration-aware routing (not document-as-raw). Completes the milestone's no-split-brain-readers clause.
